title: Modular Office — Narrow Open Shelf Base
summary: A narrow open-shelf cabinet from one ripped sheet of plywood, trimmed front and side to hide the raw edges — one module in a mix-and-match office system.
description:
This is one base cabinet in a modular office collection: a short, narrow open-shelf box you can pair with drawer bases and file bases under a shared desktop. Built from a sheet of 3/4" plywood or MDF ripped into 15-1/2" strips, it's a fast, clean build whose only real refinement is the trim that frames the front and hides the plywood edges.

The trim does double duty — it dresses the front and it reinforces the joints. When you fasten the top side trim into both the top and the sides, you're stiffening the box's corners, not just decorating them, so don't treat those pieces as optional. If you build with plywood, iron on edge banding for the front edges; with MDF you can skip it since MDF edges paint cleanly.

Because it's modular, keep it square and keep the 15-1/2" depth consistent with the other pieces in the collection — a base that's a hair off will throw the shared desktop out of flush. Pocket screws give the cleanest carcass, but screws and glue work fine.

tools.note: Recommended for a clean carcass; screws and glue also work.
Or have the store rip the sheet into 15-1/2" strips.
Attaches the back and the trim with glue.
Only if you build in plywood — hides the front edges.

materials.name: 3/4" plywood or MDF, 4x8 sheet
materials.note: Rip into 15-1/2" strips; scraps feed other modules in the collection.

materials.name: Edge banding
materials.note: Front plywood edges; skip if using MDF.

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Fasten the two shelves to the sides, then the top to the sides, with pocket screws (or screws and glue). Keep it square — this module has to line up with the others under a shared top.

steps.title: Add the back
steps.body: Glue-and-nail the 1/4" back on with 1-1/4" nails. It squares the box and keeps it rigid.

steps.title: Inside front trim
steps.body: Fasten the top and bottom inside front trim, nailing through the sides and shelves into the edges of the trim with 2" nails. This frames the opening.

steps.title: Side and front trim
steps.body: Line the side trim up with the front edge of the shelves and top and fasten with 1-1/4" nails and glue — fasten the top side trim into both the top and the sides, which reinforces the corner joints. Then measure, cut, and attach the front trim with brads and glue.

steps.title: Edge-band and finish
steps.body: If you built in plywood, iron edge banding onto the exposed front edges and trim it flush. Sand everything smooth and finish to match the rest of your office modules.

images.alt: Narrow open-shelf office base cabinet in plywood
